title: zea mays var. indentata
intro: Zea mays var. indentata, also known as wahoo or American wahoo, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its distinctive, deeply cut leaves and white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of saponins and alkaloids. The health benefits of Zea mays var. indentata are said to include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential use as a natural remedy for wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
